title: Ajax × odorus
taxonomy: Ajax × odorus is classified as a hybrid plant, specifically a hybrid of the genus Ajax. The Ajax genus belongs to the family of plants known as Gentianaceae. Ajax × odorus is further classified within the family as belonging to the order Gentianales and the class Magnoliopsida. This classification is based on the plant's botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships. Ajax × odorus is often used in horticulture and is valued for its unique and fragrant flowers.

intro: Ajax × odorus, also known as sweet cicely, is a biennial or perennial plant belonging to the Apiaceae family, characterized by its fern-like foliage and fragrant, sweet-tasting roots. The health benefits of sweet cicely include its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in treating digestive issues, wounds, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include essential oils, flavonoids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Sweet cicely is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in culinary applications like candies and desserts due to its sweet flavor.
